testcase.name: 6500-dimension-aspect-node-testcase
Dimension Aspect Node Testcase
testcase.description: Table linkbase test case using an aspect node with a dimension aspect

variation.name: Aspect node for a typed dimension
variation.description: Simple table with two rows (primary items m1, m2) and unknown number of columns (reported values of typed dimension T). In this variation there is no instance, so there are no reported values of T.

variation.name: Aspect node for a typed dimension, with instance
variation.description: Simple table with two rows (primary items m1, m2) and 3 columns (reported values of typed dimension T).

variation.name: Aspect node for an explicit dimension
variation.description: Simple table with two rows (primary items m1, m2) and unknown number of columns (reported values of explicit dimension F). In this variation there is no instance, so there are no reported values of F.

variation.name: Aspect node for an explicit dimension, with instance
variation.description: Simple table with two rows (primary items m1, m2) and 5 columns (reported values of explicit dimension F).

variation.name: Aspect node for a typed dimension on the same axis as rule nodes for a second typed dimension
variation.description: Table with two rows (primary items m1, m2), and two breakdowns on the x axis: one consisting of rule nodes for 5 values of typed dimension R and one aspect node for typed dimension T (where the reported values differ according to the value of R). This one is based on Bartosz' use case where top five countries are required. In this variation there is no instance, so there are no reported values of D.

variation.name: Aspect node for a typed dimension on the same axis as rule nodes for a second typed dimension, with instance
variation.description: Table with two rows (primary items m1, m2), and two breakdowns on the x axis: one consisting of rule nodes for 5 values of typed dimension R and one aspect node for typed dimension T (where the reported values differ according to the value of R). This one is based on Bartosz' use case where top five countries are required.

variation.name: Aspect node for a dimension that does not exist in the DTS
variation.description: Table with an aspect node for a dimension that does not exist in the DTS.

variation.name: Aspect node for a typed dimension including 'unreported value', with instance
variation.description: Simple table with two rows (primary items m1, m2) and 3 columns (reported values of typed dimension T, including 'unreported value').

variation.name: Aspect node for a dimension with no hypercubes
variation.description: Table uses a dimension with no hypercubes. This presents a potential problem for instance serialization as the dimension could go in either the segment or the scenario, but this should not prevent this table linkbase being considered valid.
